gaps: No existing test case covers EQ settings persistence on Falcon Grey (Android).
No test case covers saving and reopening EQ settings page to verify custom values are retained.
No test case covers EQ settings functionality at all across any platform or device.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ settings persist after saving and reopening on Falcon Grey (Android)
steps: 1. Launch the app on Falcon Grey (Android) device.
2. Navigate to Settings > Audio > Equalizer (EQ).
3. Verify the EQ settings page is displayed.
4. Set a custom EQ profile by adjusting at least 3 different frequency bands to non-default values (e.g., increase bass, reduce mids, boost treble).
5. Tap 'Save' or 'Apply' to save the custom EQ settings.
6. Navigate away from the EQ settings page (e.g., go back to Settings or Audio menu).
7. Reopen the EQ settings page (Settings > Audio > Equalizer).
8. Observe the EQ band values displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: All custom EQ band adjustments made in step 4 are retained and displayed correctly after reopening the EQ settings page. The values should match exactly what was saved.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ settings persist after app restart on Falcon Grey (Android)
steps: 1. Launch the app on Falcon Grey (Android) device.
2. Navigate to Settings > Audio > Equalizer (EQ).
3. Set a custom EQ profile by adjusting frequency bands to distinct non-default values.
4. Save the custom EQ settings.
5. Fully close the app (swipe from recent apps or force stop).
6. Relaunch the app.
7. Navigate to Settings > Audio > Equalizer (EQ).
8. Check the EQ band values.
generated_testcases.expected: All custom EQ band adjustments are retained after a full app restart. The EQ settings page displays the previously saved custom values.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ settings persist after switching audio output device on Falcon Grey (Android)
steps: 1. Launch the app on Falcon Grey (Android) device.
2. Navigate to Settings > Audio > Equalizer (EQ).
3. Set a custom EQ profile with specific band adjustments.
4. Save the custom EQ settings.
5. Switch audio output from speaker to Bluetooth headphones (pair a Bluetooth device).
6. Play audio through Bluetooth headphones.
7. Navigate back to Settings > Audio > Equalizer (EQ).
8. Observe the EQ band values.
generated_testcases.expected: Custom EQ settings persist and are displayed correctly after switching the audio output device. The saved EQ profile remains unchanged.

generated_testcases.title: Custom EQ settings persist after switching between EQ presets and back to custom on Falcon Grey (Android)
steps: 1. Launch the app on Falcon Grey (Android) device.
2. Navigate to Settings > Audio > Equalizer (EQ).
3. Set a custom EQ profile with specific band adjustments.
4. Save the custom EQ settings.
5. Select a different EQ preset (e.g., 'Rock', 'Jazz', or 'Classical').
6. Save or apply the preset.
7. Switch back to the 'Custom' EQ option.
8. Observe the EQ band values.
generated_testcases.expected: When switching back to the 'Custom' EQ option, the previously saved custom EQ band values are restored and displayed correctly.

generated_testcases.title: EQ settings page loads with default values on first visit on Falcon Grey (Android)
steps: 1. Ensure the app is freshly installed or EQ settings have been reset to defaults on Falcon Grey (Android).
2. Launch the app.
3. Navigate to Settings > Audio > Equalizer (EQ).
4. Observe the EQ band values without making any changes.
5. Note the default values for each frequency band.
generated_testcases.expected: The EQ settings page loads with manufacturer/app default values. All frequency bands are set to neutral (0 dB) or the defined default levels.

generated_testcases.title: EQ settings persist after device rotation on Falcon Grey (Android)
steps: 1. Launch the app on Falcon Grey (Android) device.
2. Navigate to Settings > Audio > Equalizer (EQ).
3. Set a custom EQ profile with specific band adjustments.
4. Save the custom EQ settings.
5. Rotate the device from portrait to landscape.
6. Observe the EQ band values on the rotated screen.
7. Rotate the device back to portrait.
8. Observe the EQ band values again.
generated_testcases.expected: Custom EQ settings persist and are displayed correctly after device rotation in both orientations. No values are reset or lost.
